audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel chapter 16, first-pass draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Ziba's provisions and untested accusation, Mephibosheth's disability and transport dependence, immediate property seizure, Shimei's protest and violence, Abishai's execution demand, David's restraint and providence interpretation, exhaustion, Hushai's espionage, Ahithophel's counsel, and public sexual seizure of ten concubines.

verses.restored: David passed a little beyond summit. Look, Ziba servant of Mephibosheth met him with pair of saddled donkeys, two hundred loaves, one hundred raisin clusters, one hundred summer-fruit portions, and skin of wine.
King said to Ziba, What are these for you? Ziba said, Donkeys are for household of king to ride; bread and summer fruit for young men to eat; wine for the faint in wilderness to drink.
King said, Where is son of your master? Ziba said to king, Look, he remains in Jerusalem, because he said, Today house of Israel will return kingdom of my father to me.
King said to Ziba, Look, all that belongs to Mephibosheth is yours. Ziba said, I bow; let me find favor in your eyes, my lord king.
King David came to Bahurim. Look, from there came a man from clan of house of Saul, whose name was Shimei son of Gera. He came out, cursing as he came.
He threw stones at David and all servants of king David, though all people and all mighty warriors were at his right and left.
Thus Shimei said in his cursing: Go out, go out, man of blood and worthless man.
YHWH has returned upon you all blood of house of Saul, in whose place you reigned. YHWH has given kingdom into hand of Absalom your son. Look, you are in your evil, because you are a man of blood.
Abishai son of Zeruiah said to king, Why should this dead dog curse my lord king? Please let me cross and remove his head.
King said, What is there to me and to you, sons of Zeruiah? If he curses because YHWH said to him, Curse David, who can say, Why did you do so?
David said to Abishai and all his servants, Look, my son who came from my body seeks my life; how much more now this Benjaminite. Leave him, and let him curse, because YHWH has told him.
Perhaps YHWH will see my affliction/eye, and YHWH will return good to me in place of his curse this day.
David and his men went on road. Shimei went along side of hill opposite him, cursing as he went, throwing stones alongside him and throwing dust.
King and all people with him came exhausted, and they refreshed themselves there.
Absalom and all people, men of Israel, came to Jerusalem, and Ahithophel with him.
When Hushai the Archite, companion of David, came to Absalom, Hushai said to Absalom, May king live; may king live.
Absalom said to Hushai, Is this your covenant-kindness with your companion? Why did you not go with your companion?
Hushai said to Absalom, No; whom YHWH, this people, and every man of Israel choose, to him I will belong, and with him I will remain.
Again, whom should I serve? Is it not before his son? As I served before your father, so I will be before you.
Absalom said to Ahithophel, Give counsel: what shall we do?

Ahithophel said to Absalom, Go in to concubines of your father whom he left to guard house. All Israel will hear that you have made yourself odious to your father, and hands of all with you will become strong.
They spread a tent for Absalom upon roof. Absalom went in to concubines of his father before eyes of all Israel.
Counsel of Ahithophel in those days was as when a man inquired at word of Divine. Thus was all counsel of Ahithophel both to David and to Absalom.

translation_notes: Ziba provides valuable supplies during flight, but David accepts his accusation against Mephibosheth without hearing Mephibosheth, checking accessibility barriers, or investigating evidence.
Mephibosheth depends on others to saddle transport. Disability access and servant control are central to whether he could leave Jerusalem.
David immediately transfers the entire estate to Ziba. Emergency judgment under stress still creates dispossession and requires due process.
Shimei combines political protest, accusation, cursing, stones, and dust. His physical aggression is real, but criticism of an anointed ruler is not a capital offense.
Abishai repeatedly proposes beheading critics. David's refusal is necessary restraint, not proof that all Shimei's claims are Divinely dictated.
David's interpretation that YHWH told Shimei to curse is one possible meaning-making response under collapse; it should not teach survivors that abuse is sent by Divine command.
The exhausted group stops and refreshes itself. Rest is not weakness in political crisis.
Hushai's speech is deliberate deception within espionage and civil war. Religious election language can be used strategically by competing regimes.

The ten concubines are sexually seized in public to establish dynastic possession. This is rape and political sexual violence; their consent is absent and their bodies are used as a message between men.
Ahithophel's reputation for oracle-like counsel does not make abusive counsel sacred, infallible, or ethically acceptable.
safety_note: Maximum safeguards: Do not dispossess disabled people on a caretaker's accusation, execute critics, or call abuse Divinely commanded. Verses 21-22 describe public rape of ten women as political strategy. Never refer to it as consensual sex, prophecy fulfillment that excuses perpetrators, or a punishment those women carried for David.
